description: Snake project stage-by-stage process feedback; no template changes apply until explicitly approved by Lefty.

entries.after: Strict-scope Snake brief with explicit in/out boundaries
entries.reasoning: Early explicit no-scope-creep wording reduced rework during build.
Host-terminal differences were a real delivery blocker; surfacing this in discovery was valuable.

entries.after: Explicit pre/post/error contracts and decision tables
entries.reasoning: Reduced ambiguity around resize failures and leaderboard write policy.
Improved auditability and smoother stage-5 verification.

entries.before: Task list without T-000 and chronicle links
entries.after: Task list with T-000 first and one chronicle ID per task
entries.reasoning: Made build accountability explicit before coding restarted.

entries.after: Per-task implementation decisions with evidence references
entries.reasoning: Preserved why-decisions and reduced hidden logic drift.

entries.after: Curses-first runtime with windows-curses dependency path
entries.reasoning: Most stable approach across PowerShell and Git Bash (with winpty launcher).

entries.after: Explicit verification checklist aligned with FR/NFR
entries.reasoning: Made pass/fail criteria visible and reviewable.

entries.before: Tail-to-neck mapping causing head-adjacent growth artifact
entries.after: Anchored neck/tail mapping with O-body expansion
entries.reasoning: Improved readability and expected visual behavior.

entries.after: Explicit artifact completion and feedback logging checks
entries.reasoning: Ensures process closes with reusable governance learnings.

entries.before: Adapters listed with base URL only; accepted field mask and endpoint path not specified in the spec
entries.after: Recommend: spec should declare the exact API endpoint, required field mask, and expected response schema per adapter so that wrong-endpoint defects (DEF-004, DEF-005) are caught at spec review, not post-release
entries.reasoning: DEF-004 (wrong Google Places domain) and DEF-005 (wrong endpoint searchNearby vs searchText, discarded keyword) both survived all 198 tests and were only caught in live E2E. The spec had no contract for the HTTP path or request body shape.

entries.before: let _ = industry; — industry keyword silently discarded; not caught by unit tests because tests used wiremock and never validated the request body content
entries.after: Recommend: adapter unit tests should assert on the serialised request body (textQuery field present and matches input), not just on the HTTP path and response parsing
entries.reasoning: The industry keyword was completely ignored in V1. Tests passed because they mocked the response without checking the request payload. A request-body assertion test would have caught this immediately.

entries.before: Live E2E item just checks exit 0 and PDF exists; does not assert non-zero record_count per adapter
entries.after: Recommend: live E2E checklist item should require outcome=success AND record_count>0 for at least one paid-API adapter before Stage 5 close; 0-record outcomes from mocked APIs can mask wrong-endpoint or wrong-body bugs
entries.reasoning: DEF-002 and DEF-005 both produced record_count=0 on the live run, which the checklist did not flag as a failure condition.

entries.before: try/catch wrapping credential set caused false-positive failure because competitor-spy.exe writes success output to stderr via tracing; $ErrorActionPreference=Stop triggered on stderr output
entries.after: Recommend: document in the template that on Windows, native CLI tools writing to stderr do not constitute PowerShell errors; wrap only specific $LASTEXITCODE checks, not try/catch, for external process calls
entries.reasoning: Caused two debugging iterations on the setup script that would have been avoided with this pattern documented.

entries.before: No explicit step to log feedback entries before close
entries.after: Add checklist item: 'Feedback entries written to templates/feedback.json before Stage 6 close' — this was missed in Competitor Spy V1 and had to be back-filled at V2 discovery start
entries.reasoning: Process improvement loop only works if feedback is captured while memory is fresh. Missing it at close risks losing valuable lessons.

entries.before: Bootstrap rules verify repository identity after clone but do not prompt for a new remote URL when governance mode is Project
entries.after: Recommend: when GOVERNANCE_MODE.md is set to 'Project' (not 'Template'), Stage 1 discovery must include an explicit step: ask the user for the target remote repository URL, run 'git remote -v' to show the current origin, and require the user to confirm or replace it before any commits are pushed. This prevents project code being pushed to the template repository by mistake.
entries.reasoning: In the Competitor Spy project, the cloned workspace origin still pointed to AI_Governance_Template.git. Code was being pushed to the template repo until the remote was manually repointed. This should be a mandatory Stage 1 gate, not an optional reminder.
